items.q: Is carbon dioxide the main cause of acid rain?
items.correct: No — acid rain is mainly caused by sulfur dioxide and oxides of nitrogen; CO₂ only makes rain very slightly acidic.
items.misconception: Acid rain is caused by carbon dioxide because it forms carbonic acid in water.
items.random_wrong: Acid rain is caused by oxygen dissolving in rainwater.
items.why: Students know CO₂ is acidic in water and is the famous pollutant, so they over-extend it; carbonic acid is far too weak to cause acid rain damage.

items.q: Are global warming and the ozone hole the same problem?
items.correct: No — warming is caused by greenhouse gases trapping infrared radiation; ozone depletion is a separate problem with different causes.
items.misconception: Global warming happens because the hole in the ozone layer lets extra heat in.
items.random_wrong: Greenhouse gases cool the planet by blocking sunlight.
items.why: Both are 'atmosphere problems' taught around the same time, so students fuse them into one mechanism.

items.correct: No — about 78% of dry air is nitrogen; oxygen makes up only about 21%.
items.misconception: Air is mostly oxygen because oxygen is the gas we breathe and use.
items.why: The biological importance of oxygen makes students assume abundance; unreactive nitrogen dominates the atmosphere.

items.q: The approximate percentage of nitrogen in clean dry air is:
items.why: It's 78%. “21%” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which gas is a major cause of acid rain?
items.why: Correct answer: Sulfur dioxide. “Nitrogen” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Chlorine is added to drinking water in order to:
items.why: Correct answer: Kill microbes. “Remove solid particles” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In 100 cm³ of clean dry air, approximately what volume is oxygen?
items.why: The real figure is 21 cm³. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
